channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On Feb. 15, 1951, Army Pvt. Bruno R. Orig was returning from a mission when he found his fellow soldiers under attack in what's now known as the Battle of Chipyong-ni.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Orleans coroner releases most names and cause of death for truck attack victims
item.description: The New Orleans coroner's office has identified all but one of the 14 people killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street packed with holiday revelers early on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 Tren de Aragua gang members arrested inside Texas border, governor says
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott on Friday said Texas troopers arrested four members of the notorious Tren de Aragua gang after they crossed into South Texas from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Selena's killer, Yolanda Saldívar, files for parole, begins review process
item.description: Saldívar, now 64 years-old, was sentenced to life in prison on Oct. 26, 1995, with the possibility of parole after 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Mendez, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion suspect was ‘No Labels’ voter, no 'animosity' towards Trump
item.description: New details surface about an active-duty U.S. Army soldier who blew up a Cybertruck in front of Trump Las Vegas hotel after his political background is revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mike Johnson clinches Speakership in stunning first ballot
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) was reelected to the top post in the House in a stunning floor vote on the opening day of Congress on Friday, securing the gavel on the first ballot. But the vote did not come without drama. Reps. Ralph Norman (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-employee charged after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Virginia hospital
item.description: The charges comes after the hospital said three babies were discovered with "unexplainable fractures" in late November and December of 2024. A similar incident took place the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, KTLA 5

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in South Carolina, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, KRON4

item.title: Illegal immigrant Honduran gang member kidnapped US woman, ‘giggled’ after threatening to sell organs: report
item.description: Eduardo Javier Ordonez Godoy, 35, allegedly kidnapped a woman at gunpoint, forced her to take money from an ATM and tied her to a tree on Christmas Eve, Texas police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing it again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts man finds family who sent messages in a bottle
item.description: After finding a bottle filled with messages in the sand, Douglas Turnbull was on a mission to connect the letters to their owners.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Szabo, NewsNation

item.title: It's official: 2024's holiday travel season was the busiest ever recorded
item.description: Those who were at the airport in recent weeks felt it, and the numbers back it up: 2024's holiday travel season was the busiest on record. That information came on Friday by way of the TSA, which made the claim on social media.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Ruidoso elk entangled with stool was freed
item.description: Elk becoming tangled in lawn furniture or other foreign objects has become a recurring issue in Ruidoso.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Flores, NewsNation

item.title: GOP trust in election accuracy rises after Trump's victory: Survey
item.description: Trump has publicly questioned or aired inaccurate claims about the 2020 election results, leading many within his party to also contest the legitimacy of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: The ruling hands a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden admin officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Biden honors leaders of Jan. 6 committee with civilian award
item.description: President Joe Biden awarded the Presidential Citizens Medal to 20 people, including leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional committee on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Carter's quest for Mideast peace didn't end with Camp David
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — As president, Jimmy Carter brokered the watershed peace agreement that removed Israel’s most powerful enemy from the battlefield. But he incurred the Israeli government's wrath decades later when he said its military rule over the Palestinians amounted to apartheid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans attacker had suspected bomb materials at home, reserved truck weeks ago, officials say
item.description: Law enforcement officials say the man who rammed a truck into a New Year's crowd had suspected bomb-making materials at his home and reserved the rental vehicle used in the attack weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Jim Mustian, Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man accused of choking reporter in racially-motivated attack
item.description: "The allegations in this case are very concerning," District Court Deputy DA Victoria Fazzino said. "The people have victim-safety concerns (and) community safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: A spectacular meteor shower is about to peak. How and when to watch
item.description: (KTLA) - Stargazers are in for a treat as the Quadrantids Meteor Shower, one of the year's best, reaches its peak over the Northern Hemisphere, according to NASA. This annual meteor shower is unique because it originates from an asteroid, 2003 EH1, rather than a comet.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 killed, 18 injured after plane crashes into warehouse in Southern California
item.description: Two people died and at least 18 people were injured Thursday afternoon when a plane crashed into the roof of a Southern California warehouse.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI releases new video of a suspect planting a pipe bomb near DNC offices on eve of the Capitol riot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol? Hoping to generate new tips from the public, the FBI is releasing
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trucks in NOLA attack, Vegas explosion rented on Turo; SF-based car app believes renters had no criminal record
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Celebrations to start off 2025 were dampened by two deadly incidents on New Year's Day: an attack on a New Orleans crowd and a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Governor seeks resignation of Western New Mexico University board after spending revelations
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham has asked for the immediate resignation of regents at Western New Mexico State University after revelations of wasteful spending and failures in financial oversight, according to documents released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, Fox 5

item.title: Deputies are stuck using radios with Los Angeles County's dispatch system out of commission
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The archaic computer dispatch system for the nation's largest sheriff's department remained out of commission on Thursday after crashing on New Year's Eve, forcing deputies to handle all calls by radio, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, KTLA 5

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WGN 9

item.title: Rescuers find gruesome scene at a Honolulu home after a fireworks blast kills 3, injures over 20
item.description: Emergency crews arrived to a chaotic and gruesome scene in a Honolulu neighborhood after a large New Year's firework tipped over after being lit and ignited a fiery, shrapnel-studded blast that killed three people and injured more than 20 others, several of them critically.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, ABC 4
